# Copirate Research Appendix

## Example Application: Playwright Evaluation

Use this as a shape example for a technology-evaluation output.

- Objective: need automated testing for ColdFusion projects
- Criteria: CF compatibility, ease of use, debugging tools, CI/CD integration
- Constraint: must fit FW/1 applications and the team learning curve
- Success: validate three critical user flows and integrate with GitHub Actions

Feature-matrix example dimensions:

- Compatibility
- API quality
- Network/control capabilities
- CI/CD integration
- Learning curve

## Best Practices

### Do

- Be thorough and complete each phase
- Stay objective and use explicit scoring
- Document evidence and tradeoffs
- Evaluate at least a small alternative set
- Prefer hands-on proof where feasible

### Avoid

- Rushing to a conclusion
- Ignoring community health
- Optimizing for the current week only
- Forgetting the migration path
- Dropping the resulting knowledge on the floor

## Methodology Checklist

- Objective defined
- Evaluation criteria documented
- Constraints recorded
- Success metrics explicit
- Primary sources reviewed
- Secondary sources checked
- Hands-on experiment run when needed
- Comparison matrix completed
- Architecture, performance, security, and maintenance reviewed
- Scoring rubric applied
- Risks and mitigations captured
- Alternatives documented
- Recommendation and roadmap produced
- Artifact stored and follow-ons created if needed

## Methodology Metadata

- Type: Research & Evaluation
- Complexity: Medium
- Typical duration: 2-8 hours depending on scope
- Outputs: research report, memories, implementation follow-up

## References

- Use `[TOPIC]_[DATE].md` naming for time-scoped research artifacts.
- Store durable findings when they will inform later decisions.
